item.title: Proclamation on Adjustment of Pharmaceutical and Pharmaceutical Ingredient Imports
item.description: A presidential proclamation issued pursuant to section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962, following a report from the Secretary of Commerce investigating the effects of pharmaceutical and pharmaceutical ingredient imports on United States national security. The proclamation implements adjustments to the importation of these products into the United States based on the findings of the national security investigation. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Proclamation Adjusting Tariffs on Aluminum, Steel, and Copper Imports
item.description: This proclamation modifies and strengthens previous tariff measures on imports of aluminum, steel, and copper into the United States. It amends Proclamation 9704 from March 8, 2018 regarding aluminum import adjustments, Proclamation 9705 from March 8, 2018 regarding steel import adjustments, and Proclamation 10962 from July 30, 2025. The proclamation consolidates and reinforces import adjustment policies for these three commodities through modifications to existing tariff structures and trade regulations. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Executive Order on Citizenship Verification Requirements for Federal Elections
item.description: This executive order directs federal agencies to implement procedures for citizenship verification in federal elections. The order cites authority under the Help America Vote Act of 2002 and the National Voter Registration Act of 1993, as well as the federal government's constitutional obligation to maintain republican governance. The order establishes requirements for verifying voter citizenship status in accordance with existing federal election law. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.

item.title: Memorandum on Compensation for Transportation Security Administration Personnel During Department of Homeland Security Funding Lapse
item.description: This memorandum addresses the compensation of Transportation Security Administration officers and employees during a period when the Department of Homeland Security is operating without appropriated funding. The document directs the Secretary of Homeland Security and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget to ensure that TSA personnel continue to receive payment for their work maintaining security operations at the nation's airports during the funding lapse. The memorandum identifies the continuation of air transportation security operations as a priority requiring uninterrupted staffing and compensation. Disclaimer: This content is AI generated. Use at your own risk. Please consult the original articles for accurate information.
